name: White Chocolate & Bailey's Almond Cookie Napo
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften gelatin in half a cup of Baileys Original Irish Cream for 5 minutes.
2. Dissolve the gelatin by stirring over very low heat, or microwave for 1 minute, stirring every 20 seconds.
3. If the mixture gels before it's ready to use, liquify it for 15 seconds in the microwave.
4. Whip the cream until stiff peaks form. Set aside in the refrigerator.
5. In a double boiler (over, not in, boiling water), beat egg whites and sugar with a hand-held electric mixer on high for seven minutes.
6. Working quickly, fold in chocolate chunks so as to only partially melt the chocolate, leaving small kernels of chocolate. Fold into the Baileys Original Irish Cream mixture.
7. To make Orange Sauce: Whisk together 1 cup heavy cream, 4 tablespoons sugar, 3 egg yolks, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, and 1 tablespoon fresh orange juice in a heatproof bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water (double boiler). Cook, stirring constantly, until peaks form. Remove from heat and cool by running over an ice bath.
8. Fold the Orange Sauce into the whipped cream mixture.
9. To prepare Almond Cookies Napoleon: Whisk together 2 cups sliced almonds, 1/2 cup sugar, 1 tablespoon almond extract, and a pinch of salt in a bowl. Add 3/4 cup melted butter and 3/4 cup all-purpose flour. Chill for 4 hours.
10. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Drop spoonfuls of dough onto buttered cookie sheets, forming flat cookies (about two heaping tablespoons each).
11. Bake for 3 minutes. Allow to cool on wire racks.
12. Assemble by placing some Orange Sauce between two cookies and drizzling with additional sauce as desired.
Serves: 12-16
